label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the given options is NOT true based on the passage about Bobby Moresco. First, let me go through each option one by one and check against the text. Option A: "His father did not support his work as a bartender." Wait, the passage mentions that when Moresco moved to Hollywood and worked as a waiter (not a bartender, but maybe a typo?), his father told him to stop this craziness and get a job because he had a wife and daughter. So the father didn't support his acting career or his waitressing job. But the option says "bar tender", which is different from a waiter. The text says he worked as a waiter, not a bartender. So maybe that's a trick. So option A is not exactly accurate. But maybe the question is looking for other issues. Option B: "Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs." The passage does talk about his struggles in the 70s, moving to Hollywood, working various jobs, then his brother's death, writing, then moving back, then another period of being out of work and cash until the Crash movie. So his life definitely had ups and downs before the success. So B is true. Option C: "His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets." The passage says that after his brother Thomas was murdered in 1983, he started writing as a way to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen, and that the play was based on his brother's killing. So C is true. Option D: "Moresco grew up in New York's Hell's Kitchen which is a few blocks east of Broadway." The first sentence says Hell's Kitchen is on Manhattan's West Side and lies right next door to Broadway. So Hell's Kitchen is next to Broadway, which would mean it's west of Broadway, not east. The option D says "a few blocks east of Broadway,"
